course_title: Physiotherapy Specialties: Musculoskeletal IIB (Cervical and Thoracic Spine)

learning_outcomes.description: Identify, describe, and critically appraise evidence on pathological conditions of the cervical, thoracic, and craniomandibular musculoskeletal and sensorimotor systems.
Explain and apply clinical reasoning principles in the physiotherapy management of cervical, thoracic, and craniomandibular disorders.
Perform a comprehensive, effective, culturally safe physiotherapy assessment of a cervical, thoracic spine and craniomandibular musculoskeletal clinical presentation.
Identify conditions that require interprofessional management or are outside the scope of physiotherapy, and identify appropriate referral pathways.
Demonstrate effective, tailored communication skills that meet the needs of clients, families, and other health professionals, as well as government, health insurance and legal bodies.
Safely and proficiently apply manual techniques and targeted exercise programs to manage and prevent upper quadrant musculoskeletal disorders.
Demonstrate accountability and professional responsibility in physiotherapy practice through the application of evidence-based healthcare.
Critically appraise physiotherapy management approaches and demonstrate the ability to integrate new evidence into clinical decision-making and ongoing professional development.

assessments.task_description:
Four (4) on-line quizzes worth 2% each will provide you with feedback on your understanding of key concepts in preparation for the In-Semester examination and Quiz 5 worth 12% will complete the theory covered from week 9. Each quiz will be open for one week, students must successfully complete all quizzes by the due date. The combined value of the quizzes comprises 20% of the theory component of the course. 

Inspera Assessment will be used for this assessment in PHTY3260. Further details, including an introduction to Inspera functionality, practice opportunities, and where to go for support, will be provided via Blackboard during the semester.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

2/09/2026

Individual exam schedules will be published on the Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
Case-based practical exam to demonstrate practical skill competencies and their interpretation. Students will be asked to demonstrate selected practical techniques and interpretation of findings in a clinical context. 

In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by other means such as audio recording or detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Multiple choice questions and short answer clinical scenario / problem based questions written examination that will require you to apply the material covered in lectures, required readings and practical classes.

Please note that where an assessment item requires the student to "List 'x' number" of responses (for example, "List 4 contraindications" to a specified technique) only the first 'x' responses listed by the student will be assessed. Any further responses listed will not be assessed.

Inspera Assessment will be used for this assessment in PHTY3260. 

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Problem-solving and practical examination (case based) worth 30%. This exam will be conducted in the exam period.

The practical examination is a MUST PASS component of the course. 

Practical assessment will be scheduled on-campus in a face-to-face format, during the central examination period. 

In this course the recording of an assessment (oral/practical) performance has been waived, a record of student performance will be taken by other means such as audio recording or detailed written notes that can be referred to if a remark is required at a later date.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
